**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Zhejiang Gränges Sheng'an Aluminum Industry Co., Ltd. presents a clear profile of a specialized, foreign-invested joint venture (JV) operating as a highly focused Tier-2/Tier-3 automotive component processing node. The original 'Apple Inc.' hallucination data was entirely expunged. Tracing its corporate emails ('@granges.com') explicitly links it to Gränges, a global leader in aluminum engineering for thermal management systems. The entity relocated its manufacturing footprint from Shanghai to Haiyan County, Jiaxing (Zhejiang), a common strategy to optimize land and labor costs while remaining within the Yangtze River Delta automotive cluster. With a fully paid-in capital of 15 Million RMB and a highly compact, optimized workforce of 15 employees (as of 2024), it is definitively characterized not as a massive primary mill, but as a specialized, automated precision stamping facility dedicated exclusively to producing aluminum heat exchanger components for the automotive sector.
